tweets.title: Interesting insight by Vice Admiral Raman Puri: DRDO wanted the EJ200 for the LCA, but a committee headed by an Air Marshal said no “the engine would be the GE F404”. With the EJ200, the aft fuselage would not have required significant changes. However, with the GE F404, the changes to the aft fuselage were substantial. The committee also recommended that the Kaveri engine program be closed.
tweets.content:
Interesting insight by Vice Admiral Raman Puri: DRDO wanted the EJ200 for the LCA, but a committee headed by an Air Marshal said no “the engine would be the GE F404”.

With the EJ200, the aft fuselage would not have required significant changes. However, with the GE F404, the changes to the aft fuselage were substantial. The committee also recommended that the Kaveri engine program be closed.
tweets.author: @alpha_defense

tweets.title: We often talk about defence and space together, so here’s an interesting comparison. A few days ago, Vikram-1 made its first flight. If we draw a chronological analogy with defence, it is comparable to an LCA MK1 class milestone. However, in defence, we have already invited a private-sector consortium to lead production of AMCA, a programme that, in terms of ambition and complexity, is far closer to SpaceX’s Starship (even though the design authority remains with DRDO). That’s the difference. In space, private industry is first proving itself on smaller launch vehicles before moving to heavy-lift systems. In defence, we’re asking private industry to participate in manufacturing one of our most ambitious combat aircraft programmes from the outset. This is exactly what we had been saying for years: we haven’t fed our industry the basic tech and forcing them into AMCA. This is why space has revived early success in private efforts.
tweets.content:
We often talk about defence and space together, so here’s an interesting comparison.

A few days ago, Vikram-1 made its first flight. If we draw a chronological analogy with defence, it is comparable to an LCA MK1 class milestone.

However, in defence, we have already invited a private-sector consortium to lead production of AMCA, a programme that, in terms of ambition and complexity, is far closer to SpaceX’s Starship (even though the design authority remains with DRDO).

That’s the difference.

In space, private industry is first proving itself on smaller launch vehicles before moving to heavy-lift systems. In defence, we’re asking private industry to participate in manufacturing one of our most ambitious combat aircraft programmes from the outset.

This is exactly what we had been saying for years: we haven’t fed our industry the basic tech and forcing them into AMCA. This is why space has revived early success in private efforts.
tweets.author: @alpha_defense

tweets.title: Those who thought ballistic missiles aren’t accurate. New satellite imagery reveals Iran's missile strike on Muwaffaq al-Salti Air Base in Jordan was larger and more precise Sat images analyzed by @EGYOSINT ➡️ Several additional aircraft hangars confirmed destroyed in the strike. ➡️ At least 3–4 hangars used to house US MQ-9 drones were destroyed. ➡️ US troop barracks were struck, with the imagery indicating American soldiers were killed.
tweets.content:
Those who thought ballistic missiles aren’t accurate.

New satellite imagery reveals Iran's missile strike on Muwaffaq al-Salti Air Base in Jordan was larger and more precise

Sat images analyzed by @EGYOSINT

➡️ Several additional aircraft hangars confirmed destroyed in the strike.
➡️ At least 3–4 hangars used to house US MQ-9 drones were destroyed.
➡️ US troop barracks were struck, with the imagery indicating American soldiers were killed.
tweets.author: @alpha_defense

tweets.title: Yes, dependence on the Israeli OEM for certain radar modes is a legitimate concern, and ideally the aircraft should have received the Uttam AESA from the outset. However, Uttam is planned for Batch 2, where it is also expected to feature GaN technology. Hopefully, once Virupaksha matures, we will see a GaN-based “steering” AESA transition for the Tejas Mk2 production standard as well. That said, taunts like “hangar beauty” should be taken on the chin. Manufacturing and capability development is difficult to process for those who aren’t associated with the domain. Let the import bahadurs win today’s narrative so India can win more important war of Atmanirbharta. A year from now, the picture will likely look very different.
tweets.content:
Yes, dependence on the Israeli OEM for certain radar modes is a legitimate concern, and ideally the aircraft should have received the Uttam AESA from the outset. However, Uttam is planned for Batch 2, where it is also expected to feature GaN technology. Hopefully, once Virupaksha matures, we will see a GaN-based “steering” AESA transition for the Tejas Mk2 production standard as well.

That said, taunts like “hangar beauty” should be taken on the chin. Manufacturing and capability development is difficult to process for those who aren’t associated with the domain. Let the import bahadurs win today’s narrative so India can win more important war of Atmanirbharta.

A year from now, the picture will likely look very different.
